feed.title: SAP Community - SAP S/4HANA
feed.subtitle: SAP S/4HANA Q&A in SAP Community

entry.title: How to reverse/cancel the reservation document in the subcontracting process in SAP
entry.content: How to reverse/cancel the reservation document created after the ASSIGN step in the subcontracting process in SAP or how to UNASSIGN it as UNASSIGN button is not giving any results.

entry.title: Über Report REA_RTRANS_UPLOAD keinen Datei Upload möglich
entry.content: Der Report REA_RTRANS_UPLOAD (aus Meldung 474197/2026) kann keine Datei hochgeladen werden. Es gibt kein Suche und auch bei manueller Eingabe.

entry.title: E stock is not shown in PI document via MI21 PDF output
entry.content: When we use MI21 to print out the PI document using PDF output， the item with E stock didn't display.

entry.title: E stock is not shown in PI document via MI21 PDF output
entry.content: When we use MI21 to print out the PI document using PDF output， the item with E stock didn't display.

entry.title: not able to issue material error showing batch stock not avialable
entry.content: when we create GRN batch was not active in article master , after few days batch activated on this article and there was stock in this item code without batch. we have created batch from MSC1N for this article . we are not able to issue stock due to stock is not against batch . please suggest solution how we can assign batch to this article stock . it is not allow to reverser GRN and adjustment for this article.

entry.title: Assign Doc. Type and Item Category to Value Chain - Adv. Intercompany Sales
entry.content:
We are activating US best practice activation in S5P system,

where we are getting error "Assign Doc. Type and Item Category to Value Chain - Adv. Intercompany Sales"

Please help us to resolve this issue

entry.title: DMEEX- PL_ELIXIR-O Elixir format for split payment we need to implement in xml
entry.content:
Is there already some ready format  pain.001.001.09 for  PL_ELIXIR-O (split payment)?

We copied the format from CGI_CT_V9 but then we are missing node <RmtInf><Ustrd> where there should be information in below format:

 /VAT/10n,2n/IDC/14x/INV/35x/TXT/33x

 Event 5 FI_PAYMEDIUM_DMEE_PL_ELIXIR_05 is not working.

entry.title: S_ALR_87012936 - I want to fetch planned depreciation only month wise
entry.content: I am executing T-code S_ALR_87012936, however it is showing planned & posted values both month-wise, how i can extract only planned depreciation values or how can i filter out only planned values in the dump

entry.title: SAP Business Data Cloud Seamless Planning – Data Retraction to S/4HANA
entry.content:
Hi Experts,

I'm trying to understand the technical architecture of SAP Business Data Cloud (BDC) Seamless Planning when it comes to writing planning data back to SAP S/4HANA.

From SAP documentation, the traditional approach is to use an SAC Export Job to write planning data from SAP Analytics Cloud to S/4HANA (e.g., ACDOCP). However, with BDC Seamless Planning, planning data is said to be physically stored in SAP Datasphere rather than duplicated in SAC.

My questions are:

  1. Is data retraction/write-back to S/4HANA officially supported with BDC Seamless Planning?

  2. If yes, what is the technical flow?

    • Does the SAC Export Job still work as before?

    • If so, where does it retrieve the planning data from—does SAC maintain a cache, or is the data read directly from Datasphere?

  3. Is there any additional configuration required compared to the traditional SAC Planning → S/4HANA export?

  4. Are there any SAP notes, official documentation, or architecture diagrams that explain this end-to-end process?

I've found documentation covering traditional SAC Planning retraction, but I haven't found any official guidance explaining how this works specifically with BDC Seamless Planning, where the planning data resides in Datasphere.

Any clarification or references would be greatly appreciated.

Thank you!

#BDC #Seamlessplanning #SAC #Datasphere

entry.title: Asset Useful Life Extension – Depreciation Recalculation for Fully/Partially Depreciated Assets
entry.content:
Business Requirement

We require SAP guidance on changing the useful life of existing assets and ensuring depreciation is recalculated correctly.

Scenario

We have two assets:

Asset 1

  • Capitalization Year: 2011
  • Original Useful Life: 30 Years
  • Depreciation: Still ongoing
  • Business Requirement: Reset useful life to 60 years effective 30.09.2026

Asset 2

  • Capitalization Year: 1995
  • Original Useful Life: 5 Years
  • Current Status: Asset is fully depreciated except for the remaining residual/scrap value of 5%
  • Business Requirement: reset useful life to 60 years effective 30.09.2026

Queries

  1. What is the SAP-recommended approach to change the useful life of these assets effective 30.09.2026?
  2. For the asset where depreciation is still running (Asset 1), will changing the useful life from 30 years to 60 years automatically recalculate the remaining planned depreciation prospe

entry.title: Single Payment for APP
entry.content:
Hi Team,

We are testing APP for our client's organization and while APP has been configured and running without any significant errors, I am Unable to generate a single payment for any particular vendor.

The APP run is grouping the invoices with their relevant Credit notes and Down payments and generating multiple payments instead of clearing out all these payments in one go.

Kindly suggest where I am going wrong because out of the three company codes in 1 company code it is not grouping but for the other two the payments are getting grouped as per invoice

entry.title: MRRL – Single ERS document for purchase orders containing return items
entry.content:
In the Evaluated Receipt Settlement (ERS) process, using transaction MRRL, when a purchase order includes return items, we need the system to create only one document instead of two separate documents: one invoice and one credit memo.

Currently, when transaction MRRL is executed for a purchase order containing return items, two separate documents are created, even when the “Document Selection per Purchase Order” option is selected.

We would like to avoid this behavior and generate only one document for the entire purchase order.

Is this possible in SAP standard? If so, does it require any specific configuration? Otherwise, would a custom development or enhancement be necessary?


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Error "Valuated stock becomes negative: 17- FD092200700700000D HF01 GRP_TRD
entry.content: While trying to do PGI for Delivery number 8100670737 i am getting Error "Valuated stock becomes negative: 17- FD092200700700000D HF01 GRP_TRD  -> see long text"

entry.title: UoM mismatch between Purchase Order and Inbound Delivery blocks Goods Receipt
entry.content:
When attempting to post the Goods Receipt via MIGO using "Kilogram" as the unit of measure, the following error occurs and blocks the posting:

"Inbound delivery qty cannot be less than previously GR-posted qty"

entry.title: Unable to reverse settlement in WBS through CJ88 (error message AA462)
entry.content:
We need to reverse a settlement to two WBS PL/21002/06-02 and PL/21002/06-03. Originally settlement was run through CJ8G t-code. While trying to reverse through CJ88, following error appears-

Quote -

 You cannot reverse this transaction ...

     Message no. AA462

 Diagnosis

     The asset line items belonging to this transaction were already reversed
     or are themselves reversal postings. In either case, the system does not
     allow another reversal. Reversed documents and the reversal documents
     for them are automatically marked as such, and you cannot process them
     further.

 

 Procedure

     Check your choice of document. If you really need to reverse this
     document, then you have to manually post a document with reversed
     positive/negative signs from the original posting (back out the posting)
     rather than posting a reversal.

 

Unquote

entry.title: Best Practice Activation Error/SMB99/CRTX_CRHD_LANG_O001 -Text for Work Center component :SV-CLD-PC
entry.content:
We are working on Us Best practice activation in S5P system,

We are getting error "/SMB99/CRTX_CRHD_LANG_O001 -Text for the Work Center " 

Activation logs shows "US_VAR0001 Entry does not exist in"

Attached screenshots for reference.

entry.title: Sales Order Blocked Percentage 14% of overdue open items exceeds 0% ( 1 days)
entry.content: Without Overdue Open Items, Sales Order Blocked Percentage 14% of overdue open items exceeds 10% (   1 days)

entry.title: Chart of Depreciation created with Reference and can see Depreciation areas copied
entry.content: New Chart of Depreciation created with Reference(Which has only 01,15,32 Dep.areas) and can see Depreciation areas automatically copied with 1,15,31,32,33 in table T093A, but not in Table T093. I manually created dep.areas 34 &35 with IFRS Accounting principle and this is causing issue while creating the Chart of depreciation area assignment to company code it shows the error as "Parallel crcy area (crcy type 30) missing for area 34 ch. of deprec. XXXX". How to fix it and how to assign the company code to chart of depreciation.

entry.title: REWORK TASK NOTIFICATION NOT TRIGGERED IN SOST - PO, PURCHASE CONTRACT APPROVAL WORKFLOW
entry.content:
REWORK TASK NOTIFICATION NOT TRIGGERED IN SOST - PO, PURCHASE CONTRACT APPROVAL WORKFLOW

I have maintained the email templates in Maintain Email templates app from the Predelivered templates - SWF_CRT_NOTIFY_RECIPIENTS along with the workflow scenario and activity ID based on the following community blog
SAP S/4HANA Email and Fiori launchpad Notification... - SAP Community 
My template names:
ZZ1_008000238_CRT_41 --> PO REWORK
ZZ1_008000304_CRT_41 --> Purchase contract REWORK

entry.title: PO Workflow Approval Clarification
entry.content:
Hello Team,

We need a clarification on the process of PO Workflow Approval through My Inbox in FIORI.

In one of my cases, we have designed 5 steps and at each step we have given the PO value as the distinct parameter to trigger that particular step.

When the PO is created, it was approved at the first 2 levels and then went to the inbox of 3rd level of approver, but before taking any action on the 3rd level the value of the PO was reduced to such a level that only first 2 level of approvers were required to approve.

Now the question is if PO is still in the inbox of 3rd level of approver but after value change only first 2 approver were sufficient to approve the PO (which they have already approved) then why PO is still stuck with the 3rd level of approver.

Why not the PO set as approved automatically and skipping all the higher level of approvers.
